app.title: r3sizer
app.subtitle: Precision downscaling with automatic sharpness optimization.

upload.dropOrClick: Drop an image or click to upload
upload.formats: PNG, JPEG, BMP, WebP, GIF, TIFF

sidebar.processPrompt: Process an image to see pipeline results

params.sigmaTip: Gaussian blur radius for the unsharp mask. Higher values sharpen coarser details but risk halos around edges. Lower values sharpen fine detail with fewer artifacts.
params.resizeKernelTip: Interpolation filter for downscaling. Lanczos3 is sharpest; Gaussian is smoothest; Catmull-Rom / Mitchell-Netravali are balanced cubic filters. Content Adaptive selects kernel per region (flat → Gaussian, edges → Lanczos3, etc.).
params.targetPsTip: Maximum allowed fraction of pixels with out-of-range values after sharpening. Lower = less artifacts but softer image. Higher = sharper but more clipping.
params.modeTip: Lightness sharpens only luminance (less color fringing). RGB sharpens all channels independently (stronger but may shift colors).
params.strategyTip: Uniform applies equal sharpening everywhere. Content Adaptive varies strength per region.
params.metricModeTip: Relative subtracts the baseline artifact ratio so only sharpening-induced artifacts are measured.
params.artifactMetricTip: Channel Clipping counts individual R/G/B values outside [0,1]. Pixel Out-of-Gamut counts pixels where any channel is clipped.
params.selectionPolicyTip: Gamut Only uses gamut excursion for both fitting and fallback ranking. Hybrid keeps gamut as the hard safety constraint but ranks fallback candidates by composite score (halo, overshoot, texture). Composite Only is experimental.
params.fitStrategyTip: Cubic fits a polynomial and solves analytically. Direct Search picks the best probe sample directly.
params.clampPolicyTip: Clamp clips values to [0,1]. Normalize rescales the entire image to fit.
params.probeStrengthsTip: Override the two-pass probe placement with explicit comma-separated strengths.
params.metricWeightsNote: Diagnostic only — does not affect selection

selection.polynomialRootDetail: Cubic P(s) = P₀ solved analytically. Sharpening strength is theoretically optimal given the probe data.
selection.bestSample: Fallback: best probe sample within budget
selection.bestSampleDetail: No polynomial root found in the probed range. The probe sample closest to P₀ without exceeding it was used.
selection.leastBadDetail: No probe satisfied the budget. The sample with the lowest artifact ratio was selected. Result exceeds P₀.
selection.budgetUnreachableDetail: The target P₀ cannot be met — all probed strengths produce artifacts above budget. Consider increasing P₀ or reducing output resolution.

fallback.fitFailed: Cubic fit did not converge. The 4×4 Vandermonde system returned no solution — the polynomial path was skipped entirely.
fallback.fitUnstable: Numerical instability detected (min_pivot too small). Polynomial coefficients are unreliable; direct search was used instead.
fallback.rootOutOfRange: Polynomial root fell outside [s_min, s_max]. Extrapolation is unsafe, so the best in-range probe was used.
fallback.metricNonMonotonic: P(s) did not increase monotonically with strength. The cubic model is unreliable; a direct probe sample was chosen.
fallback.budgetTooStrict: Baseline artifact ratio ({{value}}) already exceeds P₀. Even the unsharpened downscale is over budget — the content is too fine for this target resolution and threshold.
fallback.directSearchConfigured: Fit strategy is set to DirectSearch. Polynomial fitting was skipped by configuration; the best probe sample was selected directly.

robustness.title: Robustness
robustness.nonMonotonicDetail: P(s) has multiple inversions across probes. The cubic fit may not represent the true relationship between strength and artifacts.
robustness.minorNonMonoDetail: One probe inversion detected; quasi-monotonicity holds. The fit is acceptable but slightly less certain.
robustness.poorFitDetail: R² < 0.85 — the cubic does not closely track probe data. The root estimate has higher uncertainty. Adding more probes or adjusting the probe range may help.
robustness.illConditionedDetail: The min_pivot of the Vandermonde system is near zero. Probe strengths may be too close together or too sparse, causing numerical instability in the polynomial solve.
robustness.looUnstableDetail: Leave-one-out cross-validation shows that removing any single probe shifts the root estimate significantly. The selected strength is sensitive to measurement noise.

robustnessHints.monotonic: P(s) decreased at some probe — mild curve irregularity.
robustnessHints.quasiMonotonic: Multiple inversions in P(s) — cubic model is unreliable for this data.
robustnessHints.rSquaredOk: R² < 0.85 — add more probes or widen the probe range.
robustnessHints.wellConditioned: min_pivot ≤ 1e-8 — probe spacings cause numerical instability in the solve.
robustnessHints.looStable: Removing any single probe shifts s* significantly — result is noise-sensitive.

advice.optimalResultBody: The polynomial solve found an analytical root. Sharpening strength is well-calibrated for this image.
advice.budgetUnreachableBody: All probe strengths exceeded the artifact budget. Try increasing Target P(s) (e.g. from 1e-3 to 1e-2), reducing output resolution, or switching to Lightness sharpening mode.
advice.artifactsExceedTargetBody: Measured P is {{ratio}}x the target. Consider raising Target P(s) or lowering sigma to reduce sharpening intensity.
advice.veryLowSharpeningBody: Strength is below 0.02 — the image may appear soft. You can raise Target P(s) to allow more sharpening, or this image may simply not need much.
advice.nearProbeLimitBody: Selected strength is at the upper edge of probe range. Consider adding higher probe values (e.g. 0.7, 1.0) so the solver has more room to find an optimal point.
advice.highBaselineBody: The resize step alone produces {{value}}% artifacts before any sharpening. This content may be too detailed for the target resolution. Try a larger output size or a smoother resize kernel (Gaussian).
advice.considerLightnessBody: RGB mode sharpens all color channels independently, which can amplify color fringing. Lightness mode only sharpens luminance — it typically produces fewer color artifacts.
advice.poorFitBody: R² = {{value}} is below 0.85. The cubic model doesn't closely match probe data. Try adding more probe points or widening the probe range for a better fit.
advice.noiseSensitiveBody: Leave-one-out analysis shows the selected strength shifts significantly when any single probe is removed. Adding more probe samples will stabilize the result.
advice.highHaloRiskBody: {{value}}% of the image is in the halo risk zone (strong edges next to flat areas). Consider Content Adaptive strategy with reduced strong_edge gain, or lower sigma.
advice.mostlyFlatBody: Over 70% of the image is flat regions. Sharpening has little to enhance — the result should be clean. If you see noise amplification, reduce sigma.
advice.lookingGoodBody: No issues detected. The current settings appear well-suited for this image.
